title: Rajendran Pillai vs Baiju on 15 July, 2025
body: Rajendran Pillai vs Baiju on 15 July, 2025 Author: Murali Purushothaman Bench: Murali Purushothaman 2025:KER:52195 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KERALA AT ERNAKULAM PRESENT THE HONOURABLE MR.JUSTICE MURALI PURUSHOTHAMAN TUESDAY, THE 15TH DAY OF JULY 2025 / 24TH ASHADHA, 1947 OP(C) NO. 498 OF 2020 E.P NO.97/2014 IN OS NO.124 OF 2010 BEFORE THE SUBORDINATE JUDGE, KOTTARAKKARA PETITIONERS: 1 RAJENDRAN PILLAI AGED 54 YEARS S/O. NEELAKANDAN PILLAI, RESIDING AT CHARUVILA PUTHANVEEDU, CHOZHIYAKKODU MURI, VILLAGE, PUNALUR TALUK, KOLLAM - 692 310. 2 REMYA AGED 31 YEARS D/O. GEETHAKUMARI, RESIDING AT CHARUVILA PUTHANVEEDU, CHOZHIYAKKODU MURI, KULATHUPUZHA VILLAGE, PUNALUR TALUK, KOLLAM - 691 310. BY ADV SRI.S.SREEKUMAR (KOLLAM) RESPONDENT: BAIJU S/O. BHASKARAN, THETTIVILA VEEDU, ANADU MURI, MADATHARA, PEERINGMALA VILLAGE, NEDUMANGADU TALUK, THIRUVANANTHAPURAM - 695 563. BY ADVS. SRI. MATHEW B. KURIAN SRI. K.T.THOMAS THIS OP (CIVIL) HAVING COME UP FOR ADMISSION ON 15.07.2025, THE COURT ON THE SAME DAY DELIVERED THE FOLLOWING: 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 2 JUDGMENT This Original Petition is filed challenging Ext.P6 order in E.A No.147 of 2018 and E.A No.174 of 2019 in E.P No.97 of 2014 in O.S No.124 of 2010 on the files of the Subordinate Judge, Kottarakkara. The petitioners herein are the defendants in the original suit. The suit was filed by the respondent/plaintiff for specific performance of contract for sale with alternate relief for realisation of money, compensation and prohibitory injunction. The suit was partly decreed and the plaintiff was allowed to realise Rs.9,50,000/- with reasonable future interest of 6% per annum from 20.02.2010 till realisation in full from defendants and their assets. The decree was made as a charge over the schedule property of the defendants. The respondent filed Ext.P2 petition for execution of decree. The petitioners/judgment debtors 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 3 filed E.A No.147 of 2018 for excluding the building and its appurtenance from the sale proceedings of the attached property contending that they are agriculturists and therefore, entitled to get the protection under Section 60(c) of the Code of Civil Procedure ( CPC ), 1908. The petitioners also filed E.A No.174 of 2019 contending that, if 3 cents of the property along with the building thereon is sold, it would be sufficient to satisfy the decree debt. Accordingly, it was prayed that the attachment with respect to 3 cents of the property and the building situated thereon shall be vacated. 2. The respondent/decree holder filed objection to the above application. It was contended that the property attached is not an agricultural property and therefore, the petitioners are not entitled for any protection under Section 60(c) of the CPC. 3. The trial court dismissed both the 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 4 applications finding that the petitioners had not adduced any evidence to show that they are agriculturists or labourers or a domestic servant as envisaged under Section 60(c) of the CPC. The trial court also observed that eventhough the petitioners had contended that the property attached fetches more than Rs.3 Lakhs per cent, no evidence has been adduced to prove the same. The trial court found that the applications are filed as delaying tactics adopted by the petitioners to prolong the matter. 4. Heard Sri.S.Sreekumar, the learned counsel for the petitioners and Sri.K.T.Thomas, the learned counsel for the respondent. 5. Sri.S.Sreekumar, the learned counsel for the petitioners submits that the petitioners are engaged in agricultural work and they are not having any piece of land other than the plaint schedule property. The residential building of the petitioners is liable to be 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 5 excluded from the sale. It is also submitted that the property is undervalued and that the market value of the property is more than Rs.45 Lakhs. Accordingly, he prayed for setting aside Ext.P6 common order and for a direction to the Sub Court, Kottarakkara to exclude 3 cents of land and the residential building thereon from sale. 6. Sri.K.T.Thomas, the learned counsel for the respondent submits that the decree was made as a charge over the schedule property of the petitioners and that when charge is created on the property of the petitioners, they are not entitled to claim exemption under Section 60(1)(c) of the CPC. Sri.K.T.Thomas, relied on the judgment of the Division of Bench of this Court in Ammini V.K v. Vibeesh and Others [2019 (3) KHC 646] in support of the said contention. As regards the contention of Sri.S.Sreekumar that the property is undervalued, 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 6 Sri.K.T.Thomas submits that the valuation certificates produced before the trial court would show that the property is dry land having a total extent of 15 cents and would fetch Rs.75,000/- per Are and the residential building thereon would fetch Rs.12,43,661/-. 7. Having gone through the order passed by the trial court, I find that, the court on the basis of the valuation certificates, has rejected the contention of the petitioners that the property attached fetches more than Rs.3 Lakhs per cent. The trial court granted sufficient opportunities to the petitioners to pay the decree debt. The trial court is right in its finding that the petitioners are adopting delaying tactics to avoid payment of decree debt. Further, since charge has been created on the property of the petitioners, they cannot claim exemption under Section 60(1)(c) of the CPC in view of the dictum laid down in the decision in 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 7 Ammini (supra). 8. I do not find any reason to interfere with Ext.P6 order passed by the trial court in exercise of the supervisory jurisdiction of this Court under Article 227 of the constitution of India. Accordingly, the Original Petition is dismissed. Sd/- MURALI PURUSHOTHAMAN JUDGE SPR 2025:KER:52195 OP(C) NO.498 OF 2020 8 APPENDIX PETITIONER'S EXHIBITS:- EXHIBIT P1 THE TRUE COPY OF THE JUDGMENT DATED 20.12.2012 IN O. S. 124 /2010 OF THE COURT OF THE SUBORDINATE JUDGE, KOTTARAKARA. EXHIBIT P2 THE TRUE COPY OF THE EXECUTION PETITION IN O. S. 124 /2010 OF THE SUB COURT, KOTTARAKARA. EXHIBIT P3 THE TRUE COPY OF THE DRAFT SCHEDULE PRODUCED BY THE RESPONDENT. EXHIBIT P4 THE TRUE COPY OF THE E.A. NO. 147/2018 IN E.P. NO. 97/2014 IN O.S. NO.124/2010. EXHIBIT P5 THE TRUE COPY OF THE E.A. 174/2019 IN E.P. NO. 97/2014 IN O.S. NO.124/2010. EXHIBIT P6 THE TRUE COPY OF THE COMMON ORDER IN E.A. 147/2018 IN E.A. 174/2019 IN E.P. 97/2014 IN O. S. 124 /2010 OF TE SUBORDINATE JUDGE, KOTTARAKARA. RESPONDENTS EXHIBITS: NIL.
